Class Client

java.lang.Object
View.Client.Client
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Agent
public class Client extends Object implements Agent
The Client class represents the main entry point for the client application. It initializes the client state and manages the execution of actions based on user input. The client can connect to a server using either RMI or TCP protocols.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static Client
    client
    The client instance that is used to interact with the server.
    private ClientState
    state
    The current state of the client, which determines the actions that can be performed.
    static View
    view
    The view used to display the client interface and handle user interactions.

  • Field Details

    • client

      public static Client client
      The client instance that is used to interact with the server.

  • Constructor Details

    • Client

      public Client()

  • Method Details

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      The main method serves as the entry point for the client application. It parses command-line arguments to determine the connection protocol (RMI or TCP) and initializes the client state accordingly.
      Parameters:
      args - Command-line arguments passed to the application.

    • run

      public void run()
      Constructs a new Client instance. This constructor initializes the client state to a default state.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • getState

      public ClientState getState()
      Returns the current state of the client. This method provides access to the current client state, which can be used to determine the available actions and the current context of the client.
      Returns:
      The current ClientState instance representing the client's state.

    • execute

      public void execute(Action action)
      Executes the specified action on the current client state. This method synchronizes access to the client state to ensure thread safety when executing actions that may modify the state.
      Parameters:
      action - The action to be executed, which may modify the client state.

    • createAction

      public void createAction(String action, String[] args)
